title: Gru and his Robbery of Mars

content.problemStatement:
Gru has decided to steal Mars - the biggest heist in the universe ever! For this, he requires a shrink gun which is available only in Dr. Zola's lab. So first he decides to steal it by infiltrating his lab.  To do this he needs to hack into his system, but Dr. Zola is quite smart and infiltrating his lab won't be so easy. Gru is at the last step of hacking into his system and suddenly a question occurs, answering which can get him access to the lab. The question is:

You have been initially given an empty String SSS. QQQ updates will be applied on SSS and after every update, you need to tell some information about the new string SSS. Each update consists of a single string PPP, and in each update, you need to append this string PPP in front of SSS. So after each update, SSS is changed by the equation S=P+SS=P+SS=P+S (where +++ denotes string concatenation). After each update, once SSS has been updated, you need to tell the number of distinctdistinctdistinct substringssubstringssubstrings in SSS. See the sample input/output for more explanation.

Help Gru in conducting the biggest robbery of the universe!

###Input:


The first line contains QQQ (number of updates).
Next i-th of the next QQQ lines contain a string PiP_iPi​ which needs to appended in front of SSS.
###Output:
For each update, print the number of distinct substrings of SSS, in a new line.

###Constraints


1≤Q≤1051 \leq Q \leq 10^51≤Q≤105
1≤∣Pi∣≤1051 \leq |P_i| \leq 10^51≤∣Pi​∣≤105
∑i=1Q∣Pi∣≤5∗105 \sum_{i=1}^{Q} |P_i| \leq 5*10^5∑i=1Q​∣Pi​∣≤5∗105
PPP contains only uppercase characters. That is from AAA to ZZZ.
###Sample Input:
2
AB
A
###Sample Output:
3
5
###EXPLANATION:
After the first query, SSS=AB, so the distinct substrings are A, B and AB. After the 2nd query, SSS=AAB, and 2 more new distinct substrings are created: AAB, AA. therefore answer is 5 for the second query.
constraints: P contains only uppercase characters, from A to Z.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer Q. Each test case contains Q lines, each line contains a string P.
content.outputFormat: For each update, print the number of distinct substrings of SSS, in a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: After the first query, SSS=AB, so the distinct substrings are A, B and AB. After the 2nd query, SSS=AAB, and 2 more new distinct substrings are created: AAB, AA.
The distinct substrings of ABC are A, B, C, and ABC.

After the first query, SSS=AB, so the distinct substrings are A, B and AB. After the 2nd query, SSS=ABC, and 2 more new distinct substrings are created: ABC, AB. After the 3rd query, SSS=ABCD, and 3 more new distinct substrings are created: ABCD, ABC, AB.

solution.approach: We use a dynamic programming approach to solve this problem. We maintain a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] is the number of distinct substrings of the substring S[i..j]. We iterate over the string S and update the dp array accordingly. Finally, we print the sum of all elements in the last row of the dp array, which represents the number of distinct substrings of the updated string S.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * n * m) where n is the length of the string S and m is the maximum length of the string P.
